frames: The agent stands upright with arms bent, hands near its chest, and both feet on the ground.
It begins to bend its torso forward while keeping its legs mostly straight and arms bent.
The agent's torso bends further forward, and its right leg starts to lift and bend at the knee.
Its torso is sharply angled downwards, with the right leg bent and moving forward, while the left leg supports.
The agent's torso straightens slightly, and the right leg extends forward, off the ground, as arms extend.
It leans forward, bending its left leg, while the right leg is bent and lifted high behind the body.
The agent is falling forward, with its torso nearly horizontal and both legs bent and off the ground.
It is completely airborne and horizontal, with arms and legs extended, in a chaotic fall.
